description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a steep gable roof and a prominent front porch. The exterior is clad in horizontal light-colored siding, and the roof is covered with grayish shingles. The front facade features two vertically aligned windows above the porch, which is supported by decorative posts and has a small set of steps leading up to the main door. A single brick chimney rises from the roof near the center. The side elevations are simple, with minimal window openings and no additional ornamentation. The overall design is reminiscent of classic American vernacular homes, with a focus on symmetry and functional outdoor space.
description: A two-story house with a steep gable roof, front porch, and light horizontal siding, featuring symmetrical windows and a central chimney.
description_view_0: Front view showing the porch with decorative posts, main entry door, and two upper windows.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long expanse of siding, a single window, and the porch extending partially along the side.
description_view_2: Angled view highlighting the steep gable roof, chimney, and upper window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a similar long siding expanse and a single window, porch visible at the far end.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with decorative posts, steps, and a single main door
